name: History × Psychology — Full YouTube Automation Pipeline

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes: Triggers Mon/Wed/Fri at 2PM. Change days/time to match your upload schedule.

nodes.name: Get Next Topic
nodes.notes: Reads the next PENDING row from your topics sheet. Sheet columns: TOPIC | STATUS | YOUTUBE_URL | PUBLISH_DATE | PILLAR | NOTES

parameters.name: contents
parameters.value:
=[{"parts":[{"text":"You are a YouTube content creator for 'Mindset of History', a channel combining historical analysis with psychology.

Topic: {{ $json.TOPIC }}

Generate a complete video production package. Return ONLY valid JSON with these exact fields:
{
  "subject": "full name",
  "era": "historical period",
  "hook_fact": "most shocking psychological fact (1 sentence)",
  "wikipedia_search_term": "best Wikipedia search term for real photo",
  "full_narration": "complete 2000-word voiceover script structured as: HOOK(30s) > SETUP(90s) > ACT1(240s) > ACT2(240s) > REVELATION(180s) > APPLICATION(120s) > CTA(25s)",
  "veo_prompts": [
    "8 cinematic Veo prompts. Each: [era] setting, [camera movement], [lighting], [mood], no text, no watermarks, photorealistic, 16:9",
    "...",
    "...",
    "...",
    "...",
    "...",
    "...",
    "..."
  ],
  "seo_title": "YouTube title max 60 chars with psychological hook",
  "seo_title_b": "Alternative A/B test title",
  "seo_description": "500-word YouTube description with keywords, timestamps placeholder, and subscribe CTA",
  "seo_tags": ["15 relevant YouTube tags"],
  "chapters": "YouTube chapter timestamps starting at 0:00",
  "thumbnail_concept": "thumbnail design brief: subject face + psychological label + color scheme"
}"}]}]

nodes.name: Gemini — Research + Script + SEO
nodes.notes: Single Gemini call generates: full narration script, 8 Veo scene prompts, SEO title/description/tags/chapters. ~$0.003 per video.

nodes.name: Parse Gemini Response
nodes.notes: Extracts and validates Gemini JSON response. Adds scheduling timestamp.

parameters.command: =mkdir -p {{ $json.clips_dir }} && pip install notebooklm-py -q 2>/dev/null; notebooklm create '{{ $json.subject }} Psychology Analysis' 2>/dev/null; notebooklm source add 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/{{ $json.wikipedia_search_term.replace(/ /g, "_") }}' --wait 2>/dev/null; notebooklm generate video --style cinematic --wait 2>/dev/null; notebooklm download video {{ $json.clips_dir }}/notebooklm_base.mp4 2>/dev/null; echo 'NOTEBOOKLM_DONE'

nodes.name: NotebookLM — Base Video Draft
nodes.notes: Uses notebooklm-py CLI. REQUIRES: pip install notebooklm-py + one-time: notebooklm login on your server. Generates base cinematic video from Wikipedia sources.

nodes.name: Wikimedia — Get Real Photo
nodes.notes: Fetches real public domain historical photos. Completely free, no API key. Returns direct image URLs for download.

nodes.name: Download Real Photo
nodes.notes: Downloads the historical photo as binary data for use in FFmpeg.

nodes.name: Split Into Scene Prompts
nodes.notes: Splits 8 Veo prompts into individual items for batch HTTP calls to fal.ai

nodes.name: Fal.ai — Generate Video Clip
nodes.notes: Calls fal.ai Veo 3.1 API. ~$0.80 per 8-second clip. Returns video URL. Replace YOUR_FAL_AI_API_KEY with your actual key from fal.ai dashboard.

nodes.name: Wait for Generation
nodes.notes: Veo generation takes 20-60 seconds. Adjust wait time if needed.

nodes.name: Download Video Clip
nodes.notes: Downloads the generated video clip as binary. Will be merged in FFmpeg assembly step.

nodes.name: Google TTS — Neural2 Voiceover
nodes.notes: Google Cloud Neural2-D voice. Deep, authoritative narrator. ~$0.05 per video. Requires Google Cloud project with TTS API enabled. Replace access token with OAuth2 credential.

parameters.command:
=# Save voiceover
VOICEOVER_PATH="{{ $json.voiceover_path }}"
CLIPS_DIR="{{ $json.clips_dir }}"
FINAL_PATH="{{ $json.final_video_path }}"

# Create clips concat list
echo '' > /tmp/concat_list.txt
for clip in $(ls $CLIPS_DIR/clip_*.mp4 2>/dev/null | sort); do
  echo "file '$clip'" >> /tmp/concat_list.txt
done

# Add NotebookLM base video if it exists
if [ -f "$CLIPS_DIR/notebooklm_base.mp4" ]; then
  echo "file '$CLIPS_DIR/notebooklm_base.mp4'" >> /tmp/concat_list_nb.txt
fi

# Step 1: Concatenate all Veo clips
ffmpeg -y -f concat -safe 0 -i /tmp/concat_list.txt \
  -c:v libx264 -crf 18 -preset medium -r 24 -s 1920x1080 \
  /tmp/raw_video.mp4 2>/dev/null

# Step 2: Apply Ken Burns effect to real photo (10 sec)
ffmpeg -y -loop 1 -i /tmp/real_photo.jpg \
  -vf "zoompan=z='min(zoom+0.0015,1.5)':x='iw/2-(iw/zoom/2)':y='ih/2-(ih/zoom/2)':d=250:s=1920x1080" \
  -t 10 -c:v libx264 -r 25 /tmp/ken_burns.mp4 2>/dev/null

# Step 3: Merge video sources
ffmpeg -y \
  -i /tmp/raw_video.mp4 \
  -i /tmp/ken_burns.mp4 \
  -filter_complex '[0:v][1:v]concat=n=2:v=1:a=0[v]' \
  -map '[v]' /tmp/merged_video.mp4 2>/dev/null

# Step 4: Final assembly with audio
# Background music - use royalty free track if available
if [ -f /home/ubuntu/background_music.mp3 ]; then
  ffmpeg -y \
    -i /tmp/merged_video.mp4 \
    -i "$VOICEOVER_PATH" \
    -i /home/ubuntu/background_music.mp3 \
    -filter_complex '[2:a]volume=0.08,aloop=loop=-1:size=2e+09[bg];[1:a][bg]amix=inputs=2:duration=first:dropout_transition=3[audio]' \
    -map 0:v -map '[audio]' \
    -c:v libx264 -crf 18 -c:a aac -b:a 192k \
    -shortest -movflags +faststart \
    "$FINAL_PATH" 2>/dev/null
else
  ffmpeg -y \
    -i /tmp/merged_video.mp4 \
    -i "$VOICEOVER_PATH" \
    -c:v libx264 -crf 18 -c:a aac -b:a 192k \
    -shortest -movflags +faststart \
    "$FINAL_PATH" 2>/dev/null
fi

echo "ASSEMBLY_COMPLETE: $FINAL_PATH"

nodes.name: FFmpeg — Assemble Final Video
nodes.notes: Stitches: Veo clips + NotebookLM base + Ken Burns photo + voiceover + background music. Outputs 1080p YouTube-optimized MP4. Requires FFmpeg installed on n8n server.

nodes.name: Save to Google Drive
nodes.notes: Backs up final video to Google Drive. Replace YOUR_GDRIVE_FOLDER_ID with your folder ID.

nodes.name: YouTube — Upload + Schedule
nodes.notes: Uploads video as PRIVATE with scheduled publish time. Category 27 = Education. Requires YouTube Data API v3 enabled in Google Cloud Console + OAuth2 credentials in n8n.

nodes.name: Mark DONE in Sheet
nodes.notes: Updates the source row: STATUS=DONE, logs YouTube URL, publish date. Creates full audit trail.

_SETUP_INSTRUCTIONS.step1_google_sheets: Create a Google Sheet with columns: TOPIC | STATUS | PILLAR | YOUTUBE_URL | PUBLISH_DATE | NOTES. Fill TOPIC rows with your 30 video topics. Set STATUS to PENDING. Replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_ID in nodes.
_SETUP_INSTRUCTIONS.step2_gemini: Get API key from aistudio.google.com. Add as HTTP Header credential in n8n (name: geminiApiKey).
_SETUP_INSTRUCTIONS.step3_fal_ai: Create account at fal.ai. Get API key from dashboard. Replace YOUR_FAL_AI_API_KEY in the Fal.ai node.
_SETUP_INSTRUCTIONS.step4_google_cloud: Create project at console.cloud.google.com. Enable: Text-to-Speech API, YouTube Data API v3, Google Sheets API, Google Drive API. Create OAuth2 credentials and add to n8n.
_SETUP_INSTRUCTIONS.step5_notebooklm_py: On your n8n server: pip install notebooklm-py. Then run once: notebooklm login (authenticates with your Google account). After this it runs headlessly.
_SETUP_INSTRUCTIONS.step6_ffmpeg: On n8n server: apt-get install ffmpeg (Linux) or brew install ffmpeg (Mac). Verify: ffmpeg -version.
_SETUP_INSTRUCTIONS.step7_background_music: Download a free cinematic background track from pixabay.com/music (search: 'documentary cinematic'). Save as /home/ubuntu/background_music.mp3 on your n8n server.
_SETUP_INSTRUCTIONS.step8_test: Run workflow manually with ONE topic. Watch each node. First run takes ~90 minutes. Subsequent runs are fully automatic on schedule.
important_notes: Meta AI and Google Flow have NO public API — they cannot be automated. Fal.ai Veo 3.1 replaces them entirely in this pipeline.
notebooklm-py uses unofficial Google APIs and may break if Google changes internal endpoints. Pipeline continues working via Fal.ai even if NotebookLM-py fails.
YouTube API requires OAuth2 — you must authenticate your YouTube channel once in n8n credentials.
Videos upload as PRIVATE and auto-publish at scheduled time. This is required for scheduled publishing via API.
Estimated cost: ~$7 per video using 8 Fal.ai clips. Reduce to 3 clips to bring cost to ~$3/video.
